## Configuration

<a id="stud-config-init"></a>
### `stud config:init`

Interactive wizard for Git providers, work-item providers (Jira/Linear), and credentials.

- **Alias:** `stud init`
- **Syntax:** `stud config:init`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash
stud config:init
stud init
echo '{"language":"example","gitProviders":["example"],"workItemProviders":["example"],"jiraUrl":"example","jiraEmail":"example","jiraApiToken":"example","jiraTransitionEnabled":true,"githubToken":"example","gitlabToken":"example","linearApiKey":"example"}' | stud config:init --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `language` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `gitProviders` | `array` | yes | `NULL` |
| `workItemProviders` | `array` | yes | `NULL` |
| `jiraUrl` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `jiraEmail` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `jiraApiToken` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `jiraTransitionEnabled` | `bool` | yes | `NULL` |
| `githubToken` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `gitlabToken` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `linearApiKey` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Confirmation message

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-config-project-init"></a>
### `stud config:project-init`

Create or merge .git/stud.config (project key, base branch, provider, tokens, work-item provider, Linear keys, JIRA_DEFAULT_PROJECT, CONFLUENCE_DEFAULT_SPACE). Run interactively or use stud config:project-init --agent with JSON. Unknown agent keys are rejected; migration_version is reserved.

- **Alias:** `stud cpi`
- **Syntax:** `stud config:project-init`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash
stud config:project-init
stud cpi
echo '{"projectKey":"SCI","transitionId":1,"baseBranch":"develop","gitProvider":"example","githubToken":"example","gitlabToken":"example","gitlabInstanceUrl":"example","jiraDefaultProject":"example","confluenceDefaultSpace":"example","workItemProvider":"example","linearStartStateId":"example","linearTypeLabelGroupId":"example","linearTypeBranchPrefixes":"example","skipBaseBranchRemoteCheck":true}' | stud config:project-init --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `projectKey` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `transitionId` | `int\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `baseBranch` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `gitProvider` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `githubToken` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `gitlabToken` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `gitlabInstanceUrl` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `jiraDefaultProject` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `confluenceDefaultSpace` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `workItemProvider` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `linearStartStateId` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `linearTypeLabelGroupId` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `linearTypeBranchPrefixes` | `object` | yes | `NULL` |
| `skipBaseBranchRemoteCheck` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Merged project configuration (redacted) and whether the file was updated

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
updated: bool
redactedProjectConfig: array
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
updated: bool
redactedProjectConfig: array
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-config-show"></a>
### `stud config:show`

Display current configuration (global and project) with secrets redacted; safe for sharing. Use -k/--key to show one whitelisted key; -q/--quiet with --key outputs only the raw value.

- **Alias:** None
- **Syntax:** `stud config:show [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--key <key><br>`-k <key>`` | Show only this config key (whitelisted non-secret keys only) |
| `--quiet<br>`-q`` | With --key: output only the raw value (no section/labels); ignored without --key |


#### Examples

```bash
stud config:show
stud config:show --key SCI-123
stud config:show --quiet
echo '{"key":"SCI-123","quiet":true}' | stud config:show --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `key` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `quiet` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Global and project configuration or a single key value

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
globalConfig: array
projectConfig: array|null
singleKey: string|null
singleKeyValue: mixed|null
singleKeySection: string|null
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
globalConfig: array
projectConfig: array|null
singleKey: string|null
singleKeyValue: mixed|null
singleKeySection: string|null
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-config-validate"></a>
### `stud config:validate`

Validate configuration and ping configured Jira, Git, and Linear providers. Use --skip-jira, --skip-git, or --skip-linear for partial checks.

- **Alias:** None
- **Syntax:** `stud config:validate [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--skip-jira` | Skip the Jira connectivity check |
| `--skip-git` | Skip the Git provider connectivity check |
| `--skip-linear` | Skip the Linear connectivity check |


#### Examples

```bash
stud config:validate
stud config:validate --skip-jira
stud config:validate --skip-git
stud config:validate --skip-linear
echo '{"skipJira":true,"skipGit":true,"skipLinear":true}' | stud config:validate --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `skipJira` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `skipGit` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `skipLinear` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Jira, Git provider, and Linear connectivity status

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
jiraStatus: string
jiraMessage: string|null
gitStatus: string
gitMessage: string|null
linearStatus: string
linearMessage: string|null
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
jiraStatus: string
jiraMessage: string|null
gitStatus: string
gitMessage: string|null
linearStatus: string
linearMessage: string|null
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

## Jira Work Items

<a id="stud-filters-list"></a>
### `stud filters:list`

Lists all available Jira filters.

- **Alias:** `stud fl`
- **Syntax:** `stud filters:list`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash
stud filters:list
stud fl
echo '{}' | stud filters:list --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |

#### Agent JSON Output

List of Jira filters

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
filters: array
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
filters: array
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-filters-show"></a>
### `stud filters:show`

Retrieve issues from a saved Jira filter.

- **Alias:** `stud fs`
- **Syntax:** `stud filters:show <filterName>`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |
| --- |
| `<filterName>` |

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash
stud filters:show "My Filter"
stud fs "My Filter"
echo '{"filterName":"My Filter"}' | stud filters:show --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `filterName` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Issues from a saved filter (agent mode returns slim issue summaries; use items:show for full details)

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
issues: array of slim issue summaries (key, status, title, priority, url)
filterName: string
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
issues: array of slim issue summaries (key, status, title, priority, url)
filterName: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-items-create"></a>
### `stud items:create`

Creates a Jira issue. Use -p/--project, -t/--type (default Story), -m/--summary, -d/--description, --description-format (plain|markdown), --parent (sub-task), -F/--fields for arbitrary fields. Default project from JIRA_DEFAULT_PROJECT in .git/stud.config. Description from STDIN then -d. Custom required fields require interactive run.

- **Alias:** `stud ic`
- **Syntax:** `stud items:create [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--project <key><br>`-p <key>`` | Jira project key (or set JIRA_DEFAULT_PROJECT in .git/stud.config) |
| `--type <type><br>`-t <type>`` | Issue type (default: Story) |
| `--summary <text><br>`-m <text>`` | Issue summary/title |
| `--description <text><br>`-d <text>`` | Issue description (STDIN takes precedence when piping) |
| `--description-format <plain\|markdown>` | Description format: plain (default) or markdown (converts to Jira ADF) |
| `--parent <key>` | Parent issue key for creating a sub-task |
| `--fields <fields><br>`-F <fields>`` | Extra fields as key=value pairs separated by semicolons (e.g. "labels=Bug,DX;priority=High") |


#### Examples

```bash
stud items:create
stud ic
stud items:create --project SCI-123
stud items:create --type Task
stud items:create --summary "Updated text"
stud items:create --description "Updated text"
stud items:create --description-format markdown
stud items:create --parent SCI-123
stud items:create --fields "labels=DX;priority=High"
echo '{"project":"example","type":"example","summary":"example","description":"example","descriptionFormat":"example","parent":"example","fields":"labels=DX;priority=High"}' | stud items:create --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `project` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `type` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `summary` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `description` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `descriptionFormat` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `parent` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `fields` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Created issue key and URL

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
key: string|null
self: string|null
skippedOptionalFields: array|null
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
key: string|null
self: string|null
skippedOptionalFields: array|null
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-items-download"></a>
### `stud items:download`

Downloads Jira attachments for an issue or one file from a Jira attachment URL. With a key, all attachments are saved (--url is ignored). Without a key, --url is required. Uses the same Jira auth as other commands. Default directory: .cursor/stud-downloads. Agent JSON: issueKey or key, optional url, optional path.

- **Alias:** `stud idl`
- **Syntax:** `stud items:download [<key>] [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |
| --- |
| `[<key>]` |

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--url <url>` | Jira REST attachment content URL (required when no issue key); ignored when a key is present |
| `--path <dir>` | Target directory relative to cwd (created if missing); default .cursor/stud-downloads |


#### Examples

```bash
stud items:download
stud idl
stud items:download --url "https://example.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/DEV/pages/12345/Page"
stud items:download --path .cursor/tmp/SCI-123
echo '{"issueKey":"SCI-123","key":"SCI-123","url":"https://example.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/DEV/pages/12345/Page","path":"example"}' | stud items:download --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `issueKey` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `key` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `url` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `path` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Downloaded attachment paths (filename, path) and per-file errors

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
files: array
errors: array
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
files: array
errors: array
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-items-list"></a>
### `stud items:list`

Lists active work items (your dashboard).

- **Alias:** `stud ls`
- **Syntax:** `stud items:list [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--all<br>`-a`` | List items for all users (overrides default assignee filter) |
| `--project <key><br>`-p <key>`` | Filter items by a specific project key (e.g., PROJ) |
| `--sort <value><br>`-s <value>`` | Sort results by Key or Status (use --sort or -s) |


#### Examples

```bash
stud items:list
stud ls
stud items:list --all
stud items:list --project SCI-123
stud items:list --sort value
echo '{"all":true,"project":"example","sort":"example"}' | stud items:list --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `all` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `project` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `sort` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |

#### Agent JSON Output

List of work items (agent mode returns slim issue summaries; use items:show for full details)

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
issues: array of slim issue summaries (key, status, title, url)
all: bool
project: string|null
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
issues: array of slim issue summaries (key, status, title, url)
all: bool
project: string|null
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-items-search"></a>
### `stud items:search`

Search for issues using JQL.

- **Alias:** `stud search`
- **Syntax:** `stud items:search <jql>`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |
| --- |
| `<jql>` |

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash
stud items:search "project = SCI and statusCategory != Done"
stud search "project = SCI and statusCategory != Done"
echo '{"jql":"project = SCI and statusCategory != Done"}' | stud items:search --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `jql` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |

#### Agent JSON Output

JQL search results (agent mode returns slim issue summaries; use items:show for full details)

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
issues: array of slim issue summaries (key, status, title, priority, url)
jql: string
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
issues: array of slim issue summaries (key, status, title, priority, url)
jql: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-items-show"></a>
### `stud items:show`

Shows detailed info for one work item.

- **Alias:** `stud sh`
- **Syntax:** `stud items:show <key>`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |

| `<key>` |

None.

```bash
stud items:show SCI-123

```

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |

Detailed work item information

```text
issue: App\DTO\WorkItem|null
```


Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

### `stud items:start`

- **Alias:** `stud start`
- **Syntax:** `stud items:start <key>`

<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

| --- |




<a id="stud-items-takeover"></a>
### `stud items:takeover`

Takes over an issue from another user. Assigns the issue to you and switches to existing branch if found, or starts fresh if no branch exists.

- **Alias:** `stud to`
- **Syntax:** `stud items:takeover <key> [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |
| --- |
| `<key>` |

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--quiet<br>`-q`` | Non-interactive: use defaults, no prompts |


#### Examples

```bash
stud items:takeover SCI-123
stud to SCI-123
stud items:takeover SCI-123 --quiet
echo '{"key":"SCI-123","quiet":true}' | stud items:takeover --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `key` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `quiet` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Takeover result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-items-transition"></a>
### `stud items:transition`

Transitions a Jira work item to a different status. If key is not provided, attempts to detect from current branch.

- **Alias:** `stud tx`
- **Syntax:** `stud items:transition [<key>]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |
| --- |
| `[<key>]` |

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash
stud items:transition
stud tx
echo '{"key":"SCI-123"}' | stud items:transition --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `key` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Transition result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-items-update"></a>
### `stud items:update`

Updates a Jira issue's fields. Use -m/--summary, -d/--description, --description-format (plain|markdown), -F/--fields for arbitrary fields. At least one field must be provided.

- **Alias:** `stud iu`
- **Syntax:** `stud items:update <key> [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |
| --- |
| `<key>` |

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--summary <text><br>`-m <text>`` | Update the issue summary/title |
| `--description <text><br>`-d <text>`` | Update the issue description (STDIN takes precedence when piping) |
| `--description-format <plain\|markdown>` | Description format: plain (default) or markdown |
| `--fields <fields><br>`-F <fields>`` | Extra fields as key=value pairs separated by semicolons (e.g. "labels=Bug,DX;priority=High") |


#### Examples

```bash
stud items:update SCI-123
stud iu SCI-123
stud items:update SCI-123 --summary "Updated text"
stud items:update SCI-123 --description "Updated text"
stud items:update SCI-123 --description-format markdown
stud items:update SCI-123 --fields "labels=DX;priority=High"
echo '{"key":"SCI-123","summary":"example","description":"example","descriptionFormat":"example","fields":"labels=DX;priority=High"}' | stud items:update --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `key` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `summary` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `description` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `descriptionFormat` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `fields` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Updated issue key

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
key: string|null
skippedOptionalFields: array|null
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
key: string|null
skippedOptionalFields: array|null
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-items-upload"></a>
### `stud items:upload`

Uploads one or more local files as Jira issue attachments via REST multipart. Paths are relative to cwd; parent-directory segments (..) are rejected. Same Jira auth as other commands. Agent JSON: key (required), files (array of path strings). Optional inputFile when --agent (same as items:update). No --quiet; use --agent for automation.

- **Alias:** `stud iup`
- **Syntax:** `stud items:upload <key> [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |
| --- |
| `<key>` |

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--file <path><br>`-f <path>`` | Local file path relative to cwd; repeat -f/--file for multiple files |


#### Examples

```bash
stud items:upload SCI-123
stud iup SCI-123
stud items:upload SCI-123 --file doc.md
echo '{"issueKey":"SCI-123","key":"SCI-123","files":["example"]}' | stud items:upload --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `issueKey` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `key` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `files` | `array` | yes | `[]` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Uploaded attachment paths (filename, path) and per-file errors

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
files: array
errors: array
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
files: array
errors: array
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-projects-labels"></a>
### `stud projects:labels`

List Linear LabelGroups and child labels for a project team.

- **Alias:** None
- **Syntax:** `stud projects:labels [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--project KEY` | Project or team key (e.g. SCI) |
| `--groups-only` | Return only LabelGroups; omit orphan non-group labels |


#### Examples

```bash
stud projects:labels
stud projects:labels --project key
stud projects:labels --groups-only
echo '{"project":"example","groupsOnly":true}' | stud projects:labels --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `project` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `groupsOnly` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Available label groups and child labels for a project or team key

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
groups: array
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
groups: array
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

Lists all visible Jira projects.

- **Alias:** `stud pj`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

None.

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash

stud pj

```

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |

#### Agent JSON Output

List of Jira projects

```text
projects: array
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text

projects: array
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

List workflow transitions (Jira) or states (Linear) for a project.

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

None.

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |

```bash
stud projects:workflow

echo '{"project":"example"}' | stud projects:workflow --agent
```

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

Available state changes for a project or team key

## Git Workflow

<a id="stud-branch-rename"></a>
### `stud branch:rename`

Renames a branch. Can regenerate name from Jira issue key or use explicit name with --name option.

- **Alias:** `stud rn`
- **Syntax:** `stud branch:rename [<branch>] [<key>] [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |
| --- |
| `[<branch>]` |
| `[<key>]` |

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--name <name><br>`-N <name>`` | Explicit new branch name (no prefix will be added). This option takes precedence over the key argument |
| `--quiet<br>`-q`` | Non-interactive: use defaults, no prompts |


#### Examples

```bash
stud branch:rename
stud rn
stud branch:rename --name custom-branch-name
stud branch:rename --quiet
echo '{"branch":"example","key":"SCI-123","explicitName":"example","quiet":true}' | stud branch:rename --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `branch` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `key` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `explicitName` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `quiet` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Rename result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-branches-clean"></a>
### `stud branches:clean`

Clean branches using conservative auto-clean eligibility

- **Alias:** `stud bc`
- **Syntax:** `stud branches:clean [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--quiet<br>`-q`` | Non-interactive: use defaults, no prompts |


#### Examples

```bash
stud branches:clean
stud bc
stud branches:clean --quiet
echo '{"quiet":true}' | stud branches:clean --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `quiet` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Cleanup result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-branches-list"></a>
### `stud branches:list`

List local branches with status and conservative auto-clean eligibility

- **Alias:** `stud bl`
- **Syntax:** `stud branches:list`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash
stud branches:list
stud bl
echo '{}' | stud branches:list --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |

#### Agent JSON Output

List of branches with status

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
rows: array
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
rows: array
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-commit"></a>
### `stud commit`

Guides you through making a conventional commit.

- **Alias:** `stud co`
- **Syntax:** `stud commit [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--new` | Create a new logical commit instead of a fixup |
| `--message <message><br>`-m <message>`` | Bypass the interactive prompter and use the provided message for the commit |
| `--all<br>`-a`` | Stage all changes before committing (restores old behavior) |
| `--quiet<br>`-q`` | Non-interactive: use defaults, no prompts |


#### Examples

```bash
stud commit
stud co
stud commit --new
stud commit --message "Ready for review"
stud commit --all
stud commit --quiet
echo '{"isNew":true,"message":"Ready for review","stageAll":true,"quiet":true,"help":true}' | stud commit --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `isNew` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `message` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `stageAll` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `quiet` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `help` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Commit result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-commit-undo"></a>
### `stud commit:undo`

Removes the last commit and keeps changes unstaged. Refuses or prompts when the last commit is already pushed; use 'stud please' to force-push after undoing.

- **Alias:** `stud undo`
- **Syntax:** `stud commit:undo [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--quiet<br>`-q`` | Non-interactive: use defaults, no prompts |


#### Examples

```bash
stud commit:undo
stud undo
stud commit:undo --quiet
echo '{"quiet":true}' | stud commit:undo --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `quiet` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Undo result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

Automatically squash all fixup! commits into their target commits.

- **Alias:** `stud ft`
- **Syntax:** `stud flatten`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash
stud flatten
stud ft
echo '{}' | stud flatten --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Flatten result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-please"></a>
### `stud please`

A power-user, safe force-push (force-with-lease).

- **Alias:** `stud pl`
- **Syntax:** `stud please`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash
stud please
stud pl
echo '{}' | stud please --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Force push result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-push"></a>
### `stud push`

Commits like stud commit, then pushes HEAD to origin (same non-force push as stud submit preflight). After a failed push, runs or offers stud please unless disabled. CLI: --no-please (interactive/quiet). Agent JSON: pleaseFallback only (default true; false skips stud please). stud push --agent --no-please equals pleaseFallback false.

- **Alias:** `stud ps`
- **Syntax:** `stud push [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--new` | Create a new logical commit instead of a fixup |
| `--message <message><br>`-m <message>`` | Bypass the interactive prompter and use the provided message for the commit |
| `--all<br>`-a`` | Stage all changes before committing (restores old behavior) |
| `--quiet<br>`-q`` | Non-interactive: use defaults, no prompts |
| `--no-please` | After a failed normal push, do not run or prompt for stud please |


#### Examples

```bash
stud push
stud ps
stud push --new
stud push --message "Ready for review"
stud push --all
stud push --quiet
stud push --no-please
echo '{"pleaseFallback":true,"isNew":true,"message":"Ready for review","stageAll":true,"quiet":true,"help":true}' | stud push --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `pleaseFallback` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `isNew` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `message` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `stageAll` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `quiet` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `help` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Push result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-status"></a>
### `stud status`

A quick "where am I?" dashboard.

- **Alias:** `stud ss`
- **Syntax:** `stud status`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash
stud status
stud ss
echo '{}' | stud status --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Status result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-switch"></a>
### `stud switch`

Switch to a local branch by Jira item key

- **Alias:** `stud sw`
- **Syntax:** `stud switch <key> [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |
| --- |
| `<key>` |

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--sync<br>`-s`` | Run stud sync after a successful switch |
| `--quiet<br>`-q`` | Non-interactive: use defaults, no prompts |


#### Examples

```bash
stud switch SCI-123
stud sw SCI-123
stud switch SCI-123 --sync
stud switch SCI-123 --quiet
echo '{"key":"SCI-123","sync":true,"quiet":true}' | stud switch --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `key` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `sync` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `quiet` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Branch switch result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
key: string
branch: string|null
matches: array
switched: bool
needsSelection: bool
synced: bool
syncExitCode: int|null
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
key: string
branch: string|null
matches: array
switched: bool
needsSelection: bool
synced: bool
syncExitCode: int|null
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

Fetch the latest base branch and rebase the current feature branch onto it

- **Alias:** `stud sy`
- **Syntax:** `stud sync`

<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

None.

```bash
stud sync
stud sy
echo '{}' | stud sync --agent
```

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |

Sync result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text

message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

## Pull Requests

<a id="stud-pr-comment"></a>
### `stud pr:comment`

Posts a comment to the active Pull Request. Supports piping from STDIN, direct arguments, threaded replies, and optional resolve-after-reply.

- **Alias:** `stud pc`
- **Syntax:** `stud pr:comment <message> [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |
| --- |
| `<message>` |

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--reply-to <target>` | Reply to a threaded feedback target returned by pr:comments --threaded |
| `--resolve` | Resolve the targeted review thread or discussion after the reply succeeds |


#### Examples

```bash
stud pr:comment "Ready for review"
stud pc "Ready for review"
stud pr:comment "Ready for review" --reply-to github:review_thread:THREAD_ID
stud pr:comment "Ready for review" --resolve
echo '{"message":"Ready for review","replyTo":"example","resolve":true}' | stud pr:comment --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `message` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `replyTo` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `resolve` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Comment post result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
message: mixed
action: string
pullNumber: int
target: string|null
resolved: bool
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: mixed
action: string
pullNumber: int
target: string|null
resolved: bool
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-pr-comments"></a>
### `stud pr:comments`

Fetches and displays issue and review comments for the current branch's PR/MR. Use --threaded or agent JSON threaded=true for grouped conversations with action metadata.

- **Alias:** `stud pcs`
- **Syntax:** `stud pr:comments [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--threaded` | Show PR/MR feedback as threaded conversations with action identifiers and resolution state |


#### Examples

```bash
stud pr:comments
stud pcs
stud pr:comments --threaded
echo '{"threaded":true}' | stud pr:comments --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `threaded` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Pull request comments and reviews. Default agent output is flat; threaded=true returns grouped conversations.

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
default: flat shape: issueComments, reviewComments, reviews, pullNumber
threaded: when threaded=true: mode, pullNumber, conversations
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
default: flat shape: issueComments, reviewComments, reviews, pullNumber
threaded: when threaded=true: mode, pullNumber, conversations
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-submit"></a>
### `stud submit`

Pushes the current branch and creates a Pull Request. Uncommitted local changes do not block submit; you get an informational note that push/PR use commits on the branch. Use --assign-to-author to assign a newly created PR/MR to the authenticated provider user. Agent JSON: optional assignToAuthor and stageAll â€” when stageAll is true, runs stud push first (optional isNew, message, pleaseFallback; same as stud push --agent).

- **Alias:** `stud su`
- **Syntax:** `stud submit [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--draft<br>`-d`` | Create a Draft Pull Request (marked as "Draft" on GitHub) |
| `--labels <labels>` | Comma-separated list of labels to apply to the Pull Request. If a label doesn't exist, you'll be prompted to create it, ignore it, or retry with a corrected list |
| `--assign-to-author` | Assign the created Pull Request or Merge Request to the authenticated provider user |
| `--quiet<br>`-q`` | Non-interactive: use defaults, no prompts |


#### Examples

```bash
stud submit
stud su
stud submit --draft
stud submit --labels "AI-Generated,RFR"
stud submit --assign-to-author
stud submit --quiet
echo '{"stageAll":true,"isNew":true,"message":"Ready for review","pleaseFallback":true,"draft":true,"labels":"AI-Generated,RFR","assignToAuthor":true,"quiet":true}' | stud submit --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `stageAll` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `isNew` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `message` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `pleaseFallback` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `draft` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `labels` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `assignToAuthor` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `quiet` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Submit result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

## Confluence

<a id="stud-confluence-page-labels"></a>
### `stud confluence:page-labels`

Add labels to a Confluence page. Use -p/--page and -l/--labels (comma-separated).

- **Alias:** None
- **Syntax:** `stud confluence:page-labels [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--page <id><br>`-p <id>`` | Confluence page ID |
| `--labels <list><br>`-l <list>`` | Comma-separated label names (e.g. research,DX) |
| `--url` | Override Confluence base URL |


#### Examples

```bash
stud confluence:page-labels
stud confluence:page-labels --page 12345
stud confluence:page-labels --labels research,DX
stud confluence:page-labels --url
echo '{"page":"12345","labels":"AI-Generated,RFR","url":"https://example.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/DEV/pages/12345/Page"}' | stud confluence:page-labels --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `page` | `string` | yes | `''` |
| `labels` | `string` | yes | `''` |
| `url` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Labels added to the page

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
labels: array
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
labels: array
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-confluence-push"></a>
### `stud confluence:push`

Create or update a Confluence page from markdown. Use -s/--space and -t/--title for create; -p/--page for update. Content from -f/--file or STDIN (create or update); agent mode may use 'file' property. CONFLUENCE_DEFAULT_SPACE in .git/stud.config for default space.

- **Alias:** `stud cpu`
- **Syntax:** `stud confluence:push [inputFile] [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |
| --- |
| `[inputFile]` |

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--space <key><br>`-s <key>`` | Confluence space key (e.g. DEV); fallback CONFLUENCE_DEFAULT_SPACE in .git/stud.config |
| `--title <title><br>`-t <title>`` | Page title (required for create; optional for update) |
| `--file <path><br>`-f <path>`` | Path to markdown file (create or update); if omitted, read from STDIN |
| `--page <id><br>`-p <id>`` | Existing page ID to update (omit to create) |
| `--parent <id>` | Parent page ID for nesting (create only) |
| `--url` | Override Confluence base URL |
| `--status` | Page status: current (default) or draft |
| `--contact-email <email>` | Add a 'Contact: @User' block at the bottom (user resolved by email via Jira) |


#### Examples

```bash
stud confluence:push
stud cpu
stud confluence:push --space SCI-123
stud confluence:push --title "Feature documentation"
stud confluence:push --file doc.md
stud confluence:push --page 12345
stud confluence:push --parent 12345
stud confluence:push --url
stud confluence:push --status
stud confluence:push --contact-email user@example.com
echo '{"file":"doc.md","content":"# Feature documentation","space":"DEV","title":"Feature documentation","page":"12345","parent":"example","url":"https://example.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/DEV/pages/12345/Page","status":"example","contactEmail":"example"}' | stud confluence:push --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `file` | `string` | yes | `` |
| `content` | `string` | yes | `''` |
| `space` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `title` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `page` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `parent` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `url` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `status` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `contactEmail` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Created or updated page ID, title, URL, and action (created|updated)

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
pageId: string
title: string
url: string
action: string
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
pageId: string
title: string
url: string
action: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-confluence-show"></a>
### `stud confluence:show`

Fetch and display a Confluence page by ID or URL. Use -p/--page (page ID) or --url (page URL); at least one required. Body is returned as markdown. Supports agent mode with JSON input (pageId, url) and output (id, title, url, body).

- **Alias:** `stud csh`
- **Syntax:** `stud confluence:show [inputFile] [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |
| --- |
| `[inputFile]` |

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--page <id><br>`-p <id>`` | Confluence page ID |
| `--url <url>` | Confluence page URL (e.g. .../wiki/spaces/SPACE/pages/123456) |
| `--confluence-url` | Override Confluence base URL |


#### Examples

```bash
stud confluence:show
stud csh
stud confluence:show --page 12345
stud confluence:show --url "https://example.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/DEV/pages/12345/Page"
stud confluence:show --confluence-url
echo '{"page":"12345","url":"https://example.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/DEV/pages/12345/Page","confluenceUrl":"example"}' | stud confluence:show --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `page` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `url` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `confluenceUrl` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Page id, title, url, and body content (markdown)

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
id: string
title: string
url: string
body: string
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
id: string
title: string
url: string
body: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

## Documentation

<a id="stud-docs-check"></a>
### `stud docs:check`

Check that the generated command reference is up to date.

- **Alias:** `stud dc`
- **Syntax:** `stud docs:check`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash
stud docs:check
stud dc
echo '{}' | stud docs:check --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Documentation freshness check result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
message: string
path: string
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
path: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-docs-generate"></a>
### `stud docs:generate`

Generate the exhaustive command reference from runtime command metadata.

- **Alias:** `stud dg`
- **Syntax:** `stud docs:generate`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash
stud docs:generate
stud dg
echo '{}' | stud docs:generate --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Documentation generation result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
message: string
path: string
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
path: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

## System and Release

<a id="stud-cache-clear"></a>
### `stud cache:clear`

Clear the update check cache to force a version check on next command.

- **Alias:** `stud cc`
- **Syntax:** `stud cache:clear`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash
stud cache:clear
stud cc
echo '{}' | stud cache:clear --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Cache clear result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-deploy"></a>
### `stud deploy`

Deploys the current release branch.

- **Alias:** `stud mep`
- **Syntax:** `stud deploy [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--clean` | Clean up merged branches after deployment |


#### Examples

```bash
stud deploy
stud mep
stud deploy --clean
echo '{"clean":true}' | stud deploy --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `clean` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Deployment result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-help"></a>
### `stud help`

Displays a list of available commands.

- **Alias:** None
- **Syntax:** `stud help [<command>]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |
| --- |
| `[<command>]` |

#### Options

None.


#### Examples

```bash
stud help
echo '{"commandName":"config:validate","command":"config:validate","essential":true}' | stud help --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `commandName` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `command` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `essential` | `bool` | yes | `true` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Agent mode schema with all commands

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
commands: array
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
commands: array
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-release"></a>
### `stud release`

Creates a new release branch and bumps the version.

- **Alias:** `stud rl`
- **Syntax:** `stud release [<version>] [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

| Argument |
| --- |
| `[<version>]` |

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--major<br>`-M`` | Increment major version (X.0.0) |
| `--minor<br>`-m`` | Increment minor version (X.Y.0) |
| `--patch<br>`-b`` | Increment patch version (X.Y.Z). This is the default if no flags are provided |
| `--publish<br>`-p`` | Publish the release branch to the remote |
| `--quiet<br>`-q`` | Non-interactive: use defaults, no prompts |


#### Examples

```bash
stud release
stud rl
stud release --major
stud release --minor
stud release --patch
stud release --publish
stud release --quiet
echo '{"version":"example","major":true,"minor":true,"patch":true,"publish":true,"quiet":true}' | stud release --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `version` | `string\|null` | yes | `NULL` |
| `major` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `minor` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `patch` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `publish` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `quiet` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Release creation result

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>

<a id="stud-update"></a>
### `stud update`

Checks for and installs new versions of the tool.

- **Alias:** `stud up`
- **Syntax:** `stud update [options]`

<details>
<summary>Options, examples, and agent schema</summary>

#### Arguments

None.

#### Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--info<br>`-i`` | Preview the changelog of the latest available version without downloading or installing. Useful for checking breaking changes before updating |
| `--quiet<br>`-q`` | Non-interactive: use defaults, no prompts |


#### Examples

```bash
stud update
stud up
stud update --info
stud update --quiet
echo '{"info":true,"quiet":true}' | stud update --agent
```

#### Agent JSON Input

| Property | Type | Optional | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compact` | `bool` | yes | `true` |
| `info` | `bool` | yes | `false` |
| `quiet` | `bool` | yes | `false` |

#### Agent JSON Output

Update is not supported in agent mode

Default compact success shape (`{"compact":true}` or omitted):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Full success shape (`{"compact":false}`):

```text
success: true
message: string
```

Error shape: `{"success":false,"error":"string"}`

</details>
